headline: Delta Baggage Fee Hike Highlights Fuel Shock And Revenue Mix Shift
summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) has raised checked baggage fees on domestic routes, its first such increase in two years. The move is intended to help offset higher jet fuel costs linked to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The change aligns Delta with recent baggage fee adjustments at United Airlines and JetBlue Airways. For you as an investor, this highlights how a large U.S. carrier that relies heavily on domestic and international passenger traffic is responding directly to external...

headline: Delta Air Lines Flags $2 Billion Iran Fuel Hit. Why the Stock Is Flying After Earnings.
summary: Delta Air Lines stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices.

The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices.

headline: Delta CEO: Entire Industry Is Taking Action on Rising Fuel Prices
summary: Delta Air Lines Chief Executive Ed Bastian said the entire airline industry is taking steps to ease the pain from sharply higher fuel costs.  Delta, which kicked off earnings season for airlines, said its fuel costs rose $330 million in the first quarter from the prior year.  For the second quarter, the company projected its fuel costs soaring $2 billion from a year ago.

headline: Airline Stocks Soar on Falling Oil Prices and Delta’s Rosy Forecast
summary: Shares of U.S. airlines are climbing after Delta Air Lines said customers haven’t been fazed by higher flight prices and the cease-fire with Iran sent oil prices tumbling.  Delta Chief Executive Ed Bastian said strong demand for air travel hasn’t taken a hit since airlines started hiking up fares and fees to offset higher jet fuel costs.  The biggest concern for the year ahead will be long jet-fuel prices remain elevated, Bastian said.

content.title: Airline Stocks Soar on Falling Oil Prices and Delta’s Rosy Forecast
content.summary: Shares of U.S. airlines are climbing after Delta Air Lines said customers haven’t been fazed by higher flight prices and the cease-fire with Iran sent oil prices tumbling.  Delta Chief Executive Ed Bastian said strong demand for air travel hasn’t taken a hit since airlines started hiking up fares and fees to offset higher jet fuel costs.  The biggest concern for the year ahead will be long jet-fuel prices remain elevated, Bastian said.

headline: Delta earnings are proof that premium consumer is 'more resilient'
summary: Delta Air Lines (DAL) stock tries to hold onto gains ahead of Wednesday's market close after the transportation giant topped first quarter earnings estimates, despite jet fuel costs ballooning to $2.6 billion tied to the Iran war and spiking oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F). UBS equity research analyst Atul Maheswari comes on Market Domination to share his perspective on Delta's results and forecasts on premium consumer bookings.

content.title: Delta earnings are proof that premium consumer is 'more resilient'
content.description:
Delta Air Lines (DAL) stock tries to hold onto gains ahead of Wednesday's market close after the transportation giant topped first quarter earnings estimates, despite jet fuel costs ballooning to $2.6 billion tied to the Iran war and spiking oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F).

UBS equity research analyst Atul Maheswari comes on Market Domination to share his perspective on Delta's results and forecasts on premium consumer bookings.
content.summary: Delta Air Lines (DAL) stock tries to hold onto gains ahead of Wednesday's market close after the transportation giant topped first quarter earnings estimates, despite jet fuel costs ballooning to $2.6 billion tied to the Iran war and spiking oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F). UBS equity research analyst Atul Maheswari comes on Market Domination to share his perspective on Delta's results and forecasts on premium consumer bookings.

summary: Delta Air Lines topped Q1 views Wednesday, plans to cut capacity, raise fees amid surging jet fuel costs. Airline stocks rally on ceasefire.

headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Rallies 1,300 Points Amid Iran Ceasefire; This Chip Stock Breaks Out (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes finished Wednesday's trading day with solid gains after a ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.  Monolithic Power and IBD Leaderboard name Comfort Systems were among several breakouts on the stock market today.  On Wednesday, the Dow scored a 2.9% gain or more than 1,300 points, while the S&P 500 advanced 2.5%.
